reasoning:
As of early May 2025, the Federal Reserve has held rates steady across its first three meetings of the year (January, March, May). The market historically anticipated ~3-4 rate cuts for 2025. Although the Fed's March "dot plot" suggested 2 cuts, market pricing per the CME FedWatch tool heavily favors 3 or more cuts by December 2025 (roughly 75% probability of 1 percentage point or 4 cuts as of late April). 

Polymarket listed the specific proposition of "Exactly 2 rate cuts" at an 18% probability on May 1st. Post-May 7 (a widely expected hold), the market anticipates a potential hold in June as well but still expects several cuts later in the year. Because this is a precise forecast ("exactly 2" instead of "2 or more"), 18% aligns perfectly with the probability density roughly centered around 3-4 cuts, leaving the tails (0, 1) and exact middle (2) with lower probability assignments. Given no recent seismic data shifts, 18% remains the best reflection of prevailing market odds for exactly two 25-bps cuts playing out in 2025.
